title: If I Had Legs I'd Kick You | Rotten Tomatoes
text: If I Had Legs I'd Kick You: Review Summary. Rotten Tomatoes Scores: Tomatometer: 92% (226 reviews); Popcornmeter: 79% (100+ verified ratings). Critical Consensus: Critics describe this as 'a fever dream immersion into parental stress' that 'connects with thunderous force' through Rose Byrne's committed performance and director Mary Bronstein's bold vision. The film employs inventive sound design and blurs reality and fantasy to depict psychological breakdown. Audience Reception: Viewers characterize it as brutally honest rather than uplifting, offering 'an intense, deeply affecting look at motherhood' for audiences willing to engage with its challenging material. However, many found it thoroughly depressing and difficult to endure. Rose Byrne's Performance: Critics praise her 'breathtaking, bravura' work, noting she 'refuses to sand down Linda's harsher edges' while revealing vulnerability beneath fury. One reviewer highlighted her as 'never less than committed' to this 'complex, contradictory study in the perils of parenthood.' Verdict: A divisive film balancing dark comedy and psychological realism—acclaimed by critics but polarizing for general audiences seeking entertainment over existential discomfort.
